spec_match.rationale: The page now matches the ChromeStatus behaviour: Chrome 138 is the final macOS 11 support line, Chrome 139+ requires macOS 12+, and Client Hints are used only as the detection signal.

interactive.rationale: The demo has a live browser probe, scenario picker, quick boundary buttons, editable platform/version fields, and a re-rendered verdict, banner, headers, and timeline.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index describes OS version classification, the compatibility timeline, and the server-side Client Hints pattern that the concept now demonstrates directly.

edge_cases.rationale: The UI exposes macOS 10.x already-unsupported paths, macOS 11 final-line and Chrome 139 blocked paths, macOS 12+ support, non-macOS visitors, missing hints, and malformed versions.

references.rationale: The concept includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/4504090090143744 link plus the User-Agent Client Hints specification link.

design_system.rationale: Inline CSS uses shared design-system variables, button background and color overrides are paired, text/background combinations keep strong contrast, and the page is no longer a static code card.

summary: The major critique is cleared: the detector is now a hands-on classifier for the actual macOS 11 removal behaviour, with live Client Hints probing and visible failure and boundary branches.
